response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented senior engineer with strong testing culture and broad full-stack experience, but he does not meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. C++ experience is limited to a short stint at National Instruments and a thesis project, far from the required 8 years. Large-scale distributed systems exposure exists (high-throughput low-latency services at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic) but spans roughly two years, not five, and his recent work is product-focused TypeScript/Node rather than cloud compute infrastructure. There is no formal technical-leadership track record leading platform strategy across teams, which is the heart of this position.
strengths: Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus active mentoring via workshops and courses
Broad polyglot experience (TypeScript, DLang, C/C++, Java) with DevOps exposure (Docker, Kubernetes, CI infrastructure)
concerns: C++ experience is roughly one year in a professional setting versus the required 8 years
No formal technical-lead role owning platform strategy, cross-team alignment, or project delivery at scale
Recent experience is product/full-stack web development, not cloud compute platforms or large-scale infrastructure

key_factors.factor: C++ depth far below minimum requirement
Lack of formal technical leadership experience
